message:
fix(cancel): propagate truncation metadata during strengthen and fix truncate_chain cause leak

strengthen(): When a CancelReason replaces its fields with a more-severe
reason, it was copying kind, severity, timestamp, message, and cause —
but NOT the truncated flag or truncated_at_depth. This meant a
non-truncated reason that strengthened from a truncated one would lose the
truncation metadata, and vice versa. Both code paths in strengthen()
(same-kind-more-recent and higher-severity) now copy truncated and
truncated_at_depth from the winning reason.

truncate_chain(): At max_depth == 0, the returned reason preserved whatever
cause chain was already attached. Now explicitly sets cause to None at
depth 0, which is correct since truncation at depth 0 means "no cause
chain should survive."

Adds regression test: strengthen_adopts_truncation_metadata_from_winner
builds a truncated User reason, strengthens it with a non-truncated
Shutdown reason, and verifies that truncated becomes false and
truncated_at_depth becomes None (matching the winning reason's state).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
